instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
SEO Insertions on What are the Various Charges Applicable to Unit Linked Group Gratuity Plans? - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: group gratuity scheme, group gratuity scheme for employees, mortality charges, policy administration charge
Body Content:
Gratuity is often one of the most important components of every employee’s cost to the company (CTC). In many cases, companies opt for group gratuity schemes, which enables them to offer employees a lump sum benefit while they are exiting the company. This lump sum benefit is called gratuity and can prove very beneficial for both employees and employers. However, opting for a group gratuity scheme for employees involves several charges for the employee. Read on below to learn more about these charges.
Charges Applicable to Unit Linked Group Gratuity Schemes
Charges for Fund ManagementOne of the major charges associated with group gratuity schemes, fund management charges are applicable as a percentage of the total value of assets. It is calculated as a percentage of the total fund value annually and is appropriated post adjusting the Net Assets Value.In accordance with regulations issued by the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority (IRDA), the fund management charges may be increased but can never be higher than 1.5% of the total fund annually. This charge is also heightened by being subjected to different cesses and taxes as per the applicable laws.
Charges for Premium Allocation:Premium allocation charges are those charges that are levied by the insurance company to cover its miscellaneous costs and expenses, including medical expenses, underwriting expenses, distributor fees, etc. The premium allocation charges are levied from the client to offset these expenses that the insurance company would otherwise have to bear. The IRDA has set certain guidelines relating to premium allocation charges to ensure a cap on it from the fifth year. However, the premium allocation charges are significantly high in the first few years and are generally levied upfront at the beginning of the scheme.
Charges for Policy Administration:A variable charge, the policy administration charge is levied on a monthly basis. They can either remain the same or be increased at a rate that is predetermined by the insurer and the client. It is the cost that the insurance company deducts in order to run its administrative charges, including the cost of paperwork, intimation of premium, etc.
Mortality Charges:A mortality charge is levied by the insurance company as protection in case of death of the insured individual. The mortality charge is determined by the insurer upon assessing the insured individual’s life expectancy based on their age, health conditions, and gender. The mortality charge is used to compensate the insurer if the person does not live till the age assumed by the insurer and for covering a few other expenses.
Charges for Termination/Surrender:Every unit linked group gratuity scheme has a date of maturity. If the insured wish to prematurely encash these units, both partially and fully, a surrender charge is levied by the insurer. For the unit linked group gratuity scheme for employees, it is usually calculated as a percentage of the annualised premiums or a percentage of the total fund value. In accordance with IRDA guidelines, there is a cap on the maximum amount that can be charged as a termination or surrender charge. The guidelines also state that no other charge can be levied if the insured prematurely encashes their units.
Case
Mr. Jain purchased a unit linked group gratuity scheme from a prominent financial institution, which he decided to prematurely encash upon its tenth anniversary. As a result, the insurer levied a surrender or termination charge on the scheme.
In the 10 years of the policy, Mr. Jain’s investment had managed to reap returns of 15%. However, since he was prematurely encashing the units, a 2.5% surrender charge was deducted by the insurer, and he received returns amounting to 12.5%.
From the table mentioned below, it is easier to assess the surrender charges Mr. Jain would have been levied if he had encashed his units at a different time.
As is evident, the surrender charge reduces as the number of years since the inception of the unit linked group gratuity scheme increases. The sooner the units are encashed from the time of the scheme’s inception, the higher the surrender or termination charge that needs to be paid.
Conclusion
Group gratuity schemes are a beneficial tool for appeasing employees. It can ensure higher loyalty from the employees towards the company and reduce the employee attrition rate. Investing in a group gratuity scheme for employees can also prove cheaper for the company.
References:
https://securenow.in/insuropedia/what-are-various-charges-applicable-unit-linked-group-gratuity-plans/

output.final_text:
What are the Various Charges Applicable to Unit Linked Group Gratuity Plans?
A group term life insurance scheme is a type of group insurance plan that pays out death benefits to an
employee's nominee under employer-employee scheme. In the event of an employee's death, whether
by accident or natural causes, the nominee would receive the lump sum insured under the plan.
An employer decides to invest a specific amount of money in order to cover the organization's gratuity
liability. If the employer chooses unit linked group gratuity scheme, then such sum is invested in a
variety of market-linked funds to help earn returns. This lump sum amount is then used to pay gratuity
claims for employees who leave the organization. This may or may not offer life cover benefit to the
employees depending on the terms of the scheme
Charges Applicable to Unit Linked Group Gratuity Schemes
1. Charges for Fund Management
One of the major charges associated with group gratuity schemes, fund management charges
are applicable as a percentage of the total value of assets. It is calculated as a percentage of the
total fund value annually and is appropriated post adjusting the Net Assets Value.
In accordance with regulations issued by the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority
(IRDA), the current cap on fund management charges in respect of each of the segregated fund
is 135 basis points. Please note that the maximum Fund Management Charge shall be declared
by the Authority from time to time.
2. Charges for Premium Allocation:
Premium allocation charges are those charges that are levied by the insurance company to
cover its miscellaneous costs and expenses, including underwriting expenses, distributor fees,
etc. The premium allocation charges are recovered from premium amount paid by the client to
offset these expenses incurred.
3. Mortality Charges:
A mortality charge is levied by the insurance company towards cost of life cover in case of death
of the insured individual if the scheme provides for life cover. The mortality charge is
determined by the insurer upon assessing the insured individual’s life expectancy based on their
age, health conditions, and gender. The mortality charge is used by the insurer for providing the
life cover if the person does not survive the policy term.
Source
https://securenow.in/insuropedia/what-are-various-charges-applicable-unit-linked-group-gratuity-
plans/
